mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment indicates weakening labor demand and declining consumer purchasing power, which forces corporate earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the t-statistic for return difference versus baseline falls below 1.5 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer spending strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or the joint signal fails to beat single-series signals in walk-forward IC tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative skew during tightening periods, or the rolling Sharpe ratio of the signal exceeds 0.5 over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Low policy rates combined with elevated unemployment indicate aggressive monetary accommodation during economic weakness, which injects liquidity into financial markets and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward tests with p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth reflects a contraction in aggregate demand, which forces immediate earnings forecast revisions and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.0%
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered cases, or out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.01 across all rolling evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports near-term consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with payroll growth over any 12-month rolling window, with a correlation p-value exceeding 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation alongside stable industrial production forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive when inflation accelerates, or the interaction term loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in high-inflation regimes.
